configSchema.description: Configuration schema for the Arxiv Scout MCP server.

SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY.title: Semantic Scholar API Key
SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY.description: Optional: An API key for Semantic Scholar to enable higher rate limits for citation and reference lookups.

tools.name: arxiv_search_papers
tools.description: Search arXiv for papers using advanced query syntax.

annotations.usageTips: Combine field prefixes (ti: for title, au: for author) with boolean AND/OR for high-precision discovery. Use categories to narrow results to specific fields like cs.AI.

query.description: The search keywords or structured arXiv query string (e.g., 'au:vaswani AND ti:attention').

max_results.description: The maximum number of papers to return in the results list (up to 50).

categories.description: A list of arXiv category codes (e.g., 'cs.LG') or natural language subject names to filter by.

sort_by.description: The criteria used to order the search results.

date_from.description: Filter results to papers published on or after this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

date_to.description: Filter results to papers published on or before this date (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

offset.description: The number of search results to skip, used for paginating through large result sets.

output_format.description: The desired format for the tool's response output.

tools.name: arxiv_get_paper
tools.description: Fetch complete metadata for a specific arXiv paper.

annotations.usageTips: Use this tool when you have a direct arXiv ID or URL to retrieve the full abstract and metadata without performing a search.

arxiv_id.description: The unique arXiv identifier (e.g., '1706.03762') or the full arXiv.org URL.

output_format.description: Choose between human-readable markdown or structured JSON data.

tools.name: arxiv_get_papers_batch
tools.description: Retrieve metadata for multiple papers in a single call.

annotations.usageTips: This is significantly more efficient than calling arxiv_get_paper multiple times when analyzing a list of IDs.

arxiv_ids.description: A list containing up to 20 arXiv identifiers or full URLs.

output_format.description: The format of the final response (markdown or JSON).

tools.name: arxiv_download_and_extract
tools.description: Extract full text from a paper's PDF.

annotations.usageTips: Target specific sections using start_page and end_page to save context tokens. Excellent for reading the Methodology or Results sections deeply.

arxiv_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper from which to extract text.

max_characters.description: The maximum number of characters to return from the PDF to prevent context window overflow.

start_page.description: The 1-indexed page number where text extraction should begin.

end_page.description: The 1-indexed page number where text extraction should stop.

include_sections.description: Whether to include an automatically detected outline of the paper's sections.

include_references.description: Whether to include the bibliography list extracted from the end of the PDF.

tools.name: arxiv_get_references
tools.description: List the papers that this paper cites.

annotations.usageTips: Use this for 'backwards snowballing' to find the foundational research this paper relies on. Powered by Semantic Scholar.

arxiv_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper whose references you wish to explore.

limit.description: The maximum number of outbound references to retrieve.

output_format.description: The desired output format for the reference list.

tools.name: arxiv_get_citations
tools.description: List newer papers that have cited this paper.

annotations.usageTips: Use this for 'forward snowballing' to find the most recent work that builds upon this paper. Powered by Semantic Scholar.

arxiv_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper to check for inbound citations.

limit.description: The maximum number of inbound citations to retrieve.

output_format.description: The desired output format for the citation list.

annotations.usageTips: Helps in finding the exact category code (e.g., 'stat.ML') for a given research topic.

group.description: An optional arXiv group code (e.g., 'cs') or a natural language search term (e.g., 'robotics').

output_format.description: The desired output format for the category list.

tools.name: arxiv_cache_stats
tools.description: View internal cache hit rates and sizes.

annotations.usageTips: Useful for monitoring the server's performance and checking how much data is stored locally.

inputSchema.description: Schema for viewing cache statistics. Requires no input parameters.

verbose.description: Optional flag to show detailed stats.

resources.name: arXiv Taxonomy
resources.description: A full list of arXiv subject category codes and their descriptions.

resources.name: Query Syntax Guide
resources.description: A reference guide for advanced search operators like ti:, au:, and boolean logic.

resources.name: Server Info
resources.description: An overview of the server's version, tools, and citations data source.

prompts.name: summarize_paper
prompts.description: Generate a high-quality structured summary using extracted full text.

arguments.name: arxiv_id
arguments.description: The unique arXiv ID of the paper to be summarized.

prompts.name: compare_papers
prompts.description: Perform a side-by-side analysis of 2-5 arXiv papers.

arguments.name: arxiv_ids
arguments.description: A comma-separated list or JSON array of the arXiv IDs to compare.

prompts.name: literature_review
prompts.description: Synthesize a professional literature review for a research topic.

arguments.name: topic
arguments.description: The specific research topic or field to review.

arguments.name: max_papers
arguments.description: Optional: The number of papers to include in the review (default 10).

arguments.name: categories
arguments.description: Optional: Category filters to apply to the review search.
